2603.16475.title: Breaking the Chain: A Causal Analysis of LLM Faithfulness to Intermediate Structures
2603.16475.paper_summary: Schema-guided reasoning pipelines ask LLMs to produce explicit intermediate structures -- rubrics, checklists, verification queries -- before committing to a final decision. But do these structures causally determine the output, or merely accompany it? We introduce a causal evaluation protocol that makes this directly measurable: by selecting tasks where a deterministic function maps intermediate structures to decisions, every controlled edit implies a unique correct output. Across eight models and three benchmarks, models appear self-consistent with their own intermediate structures but fail to update predictions after intervention in up to 60% of cases -- revealing that apparent faithfulness is fragile once the intermediate structure changes. When derivation of the final decision from the structure is delegated to an external tool, this fragility largely disappears; however, prompts which ask to prioritize the intermediate structure over the original input do not materially close the gap. Overall, intermediate structures in schema-guided pipelines function as influential context rather than stable causal mediators.
2603.16475.paper_summary_zh: 模式引導推理管道要求大型語言模型（LLMs）在做出最終決策之前產生明確的中間結構——評分標準、檢查清單、驗證查詢。<lb/>但這些結構是因果決定輸出，還是僅僅伴隨著它？<lb/>我們引入了一種因果評估協議，使這一點可以直接測量：通過選擇一個確定性函數將中間結構映射到決策的任務，每一次控制編輯都意味著一個獨特的正確輸出。<lb/>在八個模型和三個基準中，模型似乎與其自身的中間結構保持自洽，但在多達60%的情況下，干預後未能更新預測——揭示出一旦中間結構改變，表面上的忠實性是脆弱的。<lb/>當最終決策的推導委託給外部工具時，這種脆弱性在很大程度上消失；然而，要求優先考慮中間結構而非原始輸入的提示並未實質性地縮小差距。<lb/>總的來說，模式引導管道中的中間結構作為影響性的上下文運作，而不是穩定的因果中介。
2603.16475.author: Oleg Somov et.al.
2603.16475.authors: Oleg Somov, Mikhail Chaichuk, Mikhail Seleznyov, Alexander Panchenko, Elena Tutubalina
